title: Ethereal Fashion Greenhouse Post-Processing Suite
description: The user explores a miniature fashion display scene where a translucent plant and an ornate corset are showcased under dramatic lighting. Through a multi-step control panel, the user layers and fine-tunes a chain of post-processing effects including bloom, chromatic aberration, vignette, tone mapping, color grading, and depth of field to craft a cinematic render of the scene.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 with EffectComposer from three/addons. Load DiffuseTransmissionPlant.glb and Corset.glb using GLTFLoader. Place the corset at the center on a reflective circular pedestal (MeshStandardMaterial, metalness 0.9, roughness 0.1). Position the plant slightly behind and to the left of the corset at scale 2.0 so it frames the corset like a greenhouse backdrop. Add a ground plane with a dark marble texture procedurally generated. Use three point lights: a warm key light (0xffe0b2, intensity 3), a cool fill light (0x90caf9, intensity 1.5), and a rim light (0xce93d8, intensity 2) behind the objects. Set the camera at position (0, 1.2, 3) looking at (0, 0.6, 0). Enable OrbitControls with damping. Build an EffectComposer pipeline with these passes: RenderPass, UnrealBloomPass, and ShaderPass for custom effects. Create a visible HTML control panel (fixed, right side, dark semi-transparent background) with these interactive steps the user must configure in sequence: Step 1: A slider for Bloom strength (0 to 3, default 0.5) and bloom radius (0 to 1, default 0.2). Step 2: A slider for chromatic aberration offset (0 to 0.02) implemented via a custom ShaderPass that offsets R and B channels. Step 3: A vignette toggle and darkness slider (0 to 1.5) via another ShaderPass. Step 4: A color grading section with sliders for hue shift (-180 to 180), saturation (0 to 2), and brightness (0.5 to 2.0) via a ShaderPass using HSB conversion. Step 5: A dropdown to select tone mapping mode (Linear, Reinhard, Cinematic, ACES Filmic) that updates renderer.toneMapping. Step 6: An Export Snapshot button that calls renderer.domElement.toDataURL and opens the result in a new tab. Each slider must update the corresponding pass uniforms in real time. Label each control group clearly. Animate the plant with a gentle sway using sine-based rotation on Y axis. The scene should look visually compelling even at default settings.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  bloomStrength: number,         // current bloom effect strength (0-3)
  bloomRadius: number,         // current bloom effect radius (0-1)
  chromaticAberrationOffset: number,         // chromatic aberration displacement in UV space (0-0.02)
  vignetteIntensity: number,         // vignette darkening strength (0-2)
  toneMappingExposure: number,         // scene exposure level for tone mapping (0-3)
  effectsEnabled: boolean,         // whether post-processing pipeline is active
  corsetLoaded: boolean,         // whether Corset.glb has loaded successfully
  plantLoaded: boolean,         // whether DiffuseTransmissionPlant.glb has loaded successfully
  activeEffectCount: number,         // count of effects with non-zero intensity
  cameraDistance: number,         // distance from camera to scene center
  renderPassActive: boolean,         // whether base render pass is functioning
}
